data.title: The Hole Truth
data.statement:
You are given a 2D grid where {{#}} represents solid material and {{.}} represents empty space.

The area outside the grid is considered empty ({{.}}). Any {{.}} cell on the border of the grid is therefore connected to the outside.

A <<hole>> is a connected region of {{.}} cells from which there is no path (through {{.}} cells) to any edge of the grid. In other words, the region is trapped inside the figure with no way out to the outside.

Empty cells are connected using <<4-connectivity>> (up, down, left, right -- no diagonals).

Count the number of holes in the grid.
data.stubGenerator:
read W:int H:int
loop H read row:string(100)
write answer
data.inputDescription:
<<Line 1:>> Two space-separated integers [[W]] and [[H]] -- the width and height of the grid.
<<Next [[H]] lines:>> A string of [[W]] characters, each either {{#}} or {{.}}
data.outputDescription: <<Line 1:>> A single integer: the number of holes in the figure.

lastVersion.statementHTML:
Goal

You are given a 2D grid where # represents solid material and . represents empty space.

The area outside the grid is considered empty (.). Any . cell on the border of the grid is therefore connected to the outside.

A hole is a connected region of . cells from which there is no path (through . cells) to any edge of the grid. In other words, the region is trapped inside the figure with no way out to the outside.

Empty cells are connected using 4-connectivity (up, down, left, right -- no diagonals).

Count the number of holes in the grid.
Input
Line 1: Two space-separated integers W and H -- the width and height of the grid.
Next H lines: A string of W characters, each either # or .
Output
Line 1: A single integer: the number of holes in the figure.
Constraints
3 ≤ W, H ≤ 100
There is at least one # cell.
There is at least one . cell.
Example
Input
5 5
.....
.###.
.#.#.
.###.
.....
Output
1
